prompt: In the stress-testing document there is a section on stress P&L calculation which shows a simple shock x sensitivity. In practice the stress P&L calculation is orchestrated similar to the VaR P&L calculation through the risk-engine (although there can also be a similar distinction where simple products are done by sensitivities but complex ones done by full revaluation.  Can you enhance the documentation to capture this...both in the stress process documentation as well as the risk engines...the way I like to think of it is that we have three parallel streams once for sensitivities & position reporting, one for VaR/SVaR and another for stress testing

I think the VaR Reporting and sign off document also needs to be changed to adapt to this new approach since this should be applicable across all types of reporting not just VaR...so it also need to cover sensitivities/positions, VaR/SVaR and Stress Reporting explain and sign off approach. I would also make one more change...I would remove the limit monitoring to its own process and make this a full limits process from initial setup and amendments of limits right the way through to limit monitoring.

ok that looks good. Can we now revert back to the Market Risk Policy and check that we have all of the processes to support what we say we cover per the policy. If we dont have full coverage then make a to do list so that we can review and close out any items

For New Product Approval this process is not Market Risk specific but covers all aspects so dont document right now but add it to the Risk Taxonomy implementation plan. For Exception and Breach management - do we not cover this in the limits process? Yes please update the market risk policy metadata to align to the new processes that we have now covered

ok having a look at the Market Risk Policy myself there are 2 ietsm that jump out as not being really well covered in the current processes...1) Economic Capital and 2) Aged Inventory (although we do have coverage from the perspective of the concentration limits). Can you assess yourself and come back with a recommendation
Can you also check back on the REgulatory requirements to see the specific regulatory drivers for these processes too
ok lets go with Option A, but also add a to do in the Risk Taxonomy Implementation Plan to cover off ICAAP documentation
I am not sure the regulatory driver should be in the Risk Engine document...should they not be back up in the policy doc?

also can you have a look at the beginning section of the risk-engine document as we might need to also regference the economic capital there (even though it is an extension of the VaR risk engine process)
I am not convinced about putting the Agend Inventory process insdie the limit process document, it doesnt seem to fit well there. Can you reassess and revert back with your assessment
ok lets proceed with option 1.
ok now that the market risk processes are up to date can you check down to the controls documentation to see if we need any updats given the changes we made to the process documentation
ok proceed
ok great can you assess any changes needed at L6 and L7 too
I know the ECAP is an extension of VaR but I feel it would be better to have its own deicated methodology document. Yes please proceed with the required L7 systems changes

I am revisiting the policy documentation and comparing against ICBCS. I see they also have a VaR Policy (see here: '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/policies/Market_Risk/VaR/Value-At-Risk_Policy_FINAL.md') and a Stress Policy (see here: '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/policies/Market_Risk/Stress/Market_Risk_Stress_Testing_Framework_FINAL.md'). I think we probably need a version of the equivalent policies for Meridien and then I need you to critically review the need for any other policy documents for traded market risk that might be missing.

Yes can you add the VaR Policy and Market Risk Stress Testing Policy but also add a Trading Book Boundary Policy too. For the other documents lets add a to do in the Risk Taxonomy implementation plan specifically to cover - 1) Model Risk Policy (enterprise level beyond just market risk), 2) ECAP Policy (beyond just Market Risk) and Environmental Risk Policy (beyond just market risk). We can leave out IRC Policy for now

I notice that for committees we only have terms of reference for the mlrc, we should probably add for some of the other committees relevant to market risk (even though they are not only market risk specific. Here are examples from the ICBCS ones 1) Board: '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/Committees/Board' 2) Board Risk Mgt Committee: '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/Committees/Board_Risk_Management_Committee' 3) '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/Committees/Executive_Committee' 4) '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/Committees/Risk_Management_Committee'

the terms of refewrence of these committees make reference to the Risk Appetite Statement, here is an example of one from ICBCS which we can use for Meridien bank as an example: '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/Committees/Risk_Appetite_Statement' and the Framework: '/Users/gavinslater/projects/riskagent/data/ICBCS Bank/Committees/Risk_Appetite_Framework'

I notice that the risk appetite numbers are shown in £ rather than $. Also, the Risk Appetite Framework also mentiones EaR which we did not account for in our Traded Market Risk processes and controls...it is aligned to Economic Capital (just another scaling factor) so it should probably be mentioned alongside the Economic Capiutal as another measure to calculate
Does the Risk-Engine document need any updating for EAR?
yes proceed
I dont believe there is any new process involved but just in case can you check the market risk process orchestration document too
yes please make the minor updates

I know you had mentioned that the Risk Appetite number were in £ becasue Meridian is a UK bank but if I recall correctly we agreed to have it reporting in $ (please check the risk limits that were already set in $)...can you check the numbers in the Risk Appetite Framework & Statement for consistency with the limits information already captured
